summary: Consistent naming, explicit typing, and narrow scoping improve readability, reduce bugs, and align with .NET Framework Design Guidelines for VB.NET.
reasoning: Option Strict On catches implicit narrowing conversions and late binding at compile time, preventing runtime InvalidCastExceptions.
PascalCase for public members and camelCase for private/local members follows the .NET Framework Design Guidelines, making code recognizable to all .NET developers.
Descriptive names reduce cognitive load during code reviews and long-term maintenance.
Narrow scoping minimizes the surface area for unintended side effects and makes refactoring safer.
